observation: {"note":"Constructing multiple generators from the same explicit seed reproduces the same stream by design. Not a multiprocessing bug."}
{"note":"Children inherited equivalent legacy rng state in this local fork experiment. Not claiming universal behavior."}
{"note":"Explicit generator is easier to reason about, but a generator present before fork can still be copied into children. Generator itself is not defective."}
{"note":"SeedSequence spawning is documented for parallel use; tiny sample does not certify statistical behavior."}
{"note":"Fresh interpreter does not create distinct streams when code supplies same seed to every worker. Not claiming spawn is ineffective."}
{"note":"Exact reproducibility across two runs does not prove reproducibility across every numpy version, OS, architecture, or pipeline."}
